role_title: Program Manager, Procurement Center of Excellence

location.office_time_required: 3 days in the office per week

level_signals: 7–10+ years of experience in project/program management or related fields
Leads complex, cross-functional transformation programs spanning multiple verticals
Drives executive-ready communications and leads governance rhythms

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Lead cross-functional procurement transformation programs spanning Strategic Sourcing, P2P, Extended Workforce, T&E, Corporate Card, Risk, and Procurement Systems.

Develop and maintain the Procurement transformation roadmap, partnering across teams to identify opportunities, prioritize initiatives, map dependencies, sequence work effectively, and align investments against strategic business objectives.
Translate business needs into scalable requirements, partnering with Procurement SMEs and EPT to design processes, workflows, controls, reporting, and user experiences.
Contribute directly to project delivery, including requirements gathering, process design, testing coordination, documentation, rollout planning, training, and change enablement.
Lead governance and operating rhythms, including project reviews, leadership updates, status reporting, issue escalation, and decision tracking.
Develop executive-ready communications and reporting, translating complex programs into clear updates, recommendations, risks, and outcomes.
Define success metrics and measure outcomes, leveraging data and insights to evaluate adoption, operational performance, process effectiveness, and business impact.
Excel at turning ambiguity into action, creating clarity and momentum in environments where priorities, requirements, and timelines evolve quickly.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7–10+ years of experience in project/program management, business operations, procurement operations or strategic sourcing, process transformation, consulting, or related fields.
Demonstrated success leading complex cross-functional initiatives involving multiple stakeholders and competing priorities.
Experience translating business requirements into scalable operational processes, system enhancements, or technology solutions.
Strong understanding of procurement, purchasing, sourcing, finance, risk, legal, or operational workflows.
Experience partnering with technology teams to design, implement, and scale business processes and systems.
Proven ability to drive execution in high-growth, rapidly changing, and ambiguous environments.
Experience with procurement and enterprise platforms such as Zip, Oracle, OneTrust, Navan, Brex, VNDLY, Salesforce, or similar systems.
Experience supporting change management, enablement, training, and organizational adoption efforts.
Enthusiasm for building scalable processes, enabling operational excellence, and leveraging AI and automation to improve business outcomes.

scope.surface: Procurement Center of Excellence — process design, governance, systems, and operating models across Strategic Sourcing, P2P, Extended Workforce, T&E, Corporate Card, and Risk
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption of new procurement processes and systems
Delivery of transformation initiatives on time
core_responsibilities: Lead cross-functional procurement transformation programs spanning Strategic Sourcing, P2P, Extended Workforce, T&E, Corporate Card, Risk, and Procurement Systems
Develop and maintain the Procurement transformation roadmap
Translate business needs into scalable requirements
Contribute directly to project delivery including requirements gathering, process design, testing coordination, documentation, rollout planning, training, and change enablement
Coordinate cross-functional implementations involving Procurement, Legal, Finance, Security, Privacy, Risk, and Enterprise Platform Technology teams
Define success metrics and measure outcomes

green_flags: Clear team mission and role scope within a well-defined Procurement COE structure
Hybrid 3 days/week with relocation assistance offered
Role spans strategic and tactical work — variety in day-to-day responsibilities
AI and automation are explicitly valued, not just buzzwords in context of a real operational role
All qualifications listed under 'Preferred' — no hard must-have language creates more accessible bar
red_flags: Compensation not disclosed anywhere in the JD
Visa sponsorship status is not mentioned
All qualifications are framed as 'Preferred' which may indicate the actual bar is unclear or the role is broadly defined
No mention of team size or reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7–10+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from internal procurement operations context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated directly)
ambiguities: All qualifications are listed under 'Preferred Qualifications' — none are explicitly labeled as 'Required'. Per extraction rules, items under 'Preferred' should be nice_to_haves. However, behavioral traits in 'You might thrive in this role if you' section read as implicit requirements for the role. Those traits have been classified as must_haves with hard severity given the unconditional framing in a 'What we look for'-style section.

The 7–10+ years experience requirement is listed under Preferred Qualifications, which formally makes it a soft requirement, but has been captured as a nice_to_have with a minimum_value of 7 per the JD text.
